messages.content:
What could cause learners to experience any barriers to learning?
Select one:
a. Being intentional with your lesson planning
b. Scaffolding instruction to grade level standards
c. Teacher lesson reflection
d. Language of learning and teaching
d. Language of learning and teaching

What is the purpose of Project-Based Learning (PBL)
Select one:
a. To promote social and communication skills by creating a classroom environment that emphasizes collaboration and exchange of ideas
b. To provide experience in and appreciation for multiple perspectives (evaluation of alternative solutions).
c. To allow learners to learn by doing, applying ideas and solve problems
d. To allow learners to construct knowledge rather than just passively take in information.
All of the options mentioned (option a, b, c, and d) are purposes of Project-Based Learning (PBL).

Question 4
Not yet answeredMarked out of 1Not flaggedFlag question
Question text
Which level of planning does learning programme fall under?
Select one:
a. Second level
b. Fourth level
c. First level
d. Third level
c. First level

A peer assessment is:
Select one:
a. An assessment where the same evidence is gathered for all learners in the same way at the same time.
b. A type of assessment where learners assess themselves against given criteria that may be reflected on as a self-checklist.
c. An assessment that provides an opportunity for learners, teachers and caregivers to acknowledge a learner's progress and achievement.
d. A type of assessment where learners assess one another in pairs or groups
d. A type of assessment where learners assess one another in pairs or groups

a. Word processing software
b. USB storage
c. Chip programming software
d. Model boats and aeroplanes
It seems that you have listed several options without any specific context or question. Could you please provide more information or clarify your question so that I can assist you better?

Possible structures projects can be made using locally sourced resources such as:
Select one:
a. Word processing software
b. USB storage
c. Chip programming software
d. Model boats and aeroplanes
d. Model boats and aeroplanes

What is the purpose of atmosphere and thermal comfort in classroom management?
Select one:
a. To create a positive and supportive learning environment that promotes students' physical and emotional well-being, and helps them learn effectively.
b. To create a quiet and sterile learning environment that minimizes distractions.
c. To create a competitive learning environment that motivates students to outperform each other.
d. To create a challenging learning environment that pushes students to their limits.
a. To create a positive and supportive learning environment that promotes students' physical and emotional well-being, and helps them learn effectively.

Which statement is untrue about an inclusive approach to learners' needs for ideal classroom planning
Select one:
a. The teacher must Offer one mode of instruction
b. The teacher must involve learners by recognizing the unique needs and strengths of each learner
c. The teacher must foster a positive and respectful classroom culture
d. The teacher must get to know their students
a. The teacher must offer one mode of instruction

What does creating respect for resources in schools mean?
Select one:
a. Teaching learners to buy their own resources for practical work.
b. Depriving learners of resources for the sake of saving the environment.
c. Teaching learners to build small prototypes.
d. Developing an awareness of the consequences.
d. Developing an awareness of the consequences.

Not flaggedFlag question
Question text
Which statement is untrue about the desirable characteristics that a Technology teacher should have?
Select one:
a. A positivist view about how technology can improve life for global society.
b. An entrepreneurial spirit or desire who wants use the learners' prototype models to make money for their benefit.
c. A pragmatic interest in why technology sometimes fails society.
b. An entrepreneurial spirit or desire who wants use the learners' prototype models to make money for their benefit.

Understanding the need for careful planning of practical tasks involves:
Select one:
a. Ignoring risks and hazards
b. Not keeping stock of materials as they are consumed.
c. Not assigning roles and responsibilities
d. Keeping logbooks of the stages of a technological activity for reflectio
d. Keeping logbooks of the stages of a technological activity for reflection

Which statement is true about resources of management
Select one:
a. Using natural light intelligently.
b. Using as much water as you want and not worrying about any leakages
c. Leaving lights on for the next class that will use the workshop.
d. Printing handouts one sided to save time.
a. Using natural light intelligently.

What is the importance of classroom management?
Select one:
a. To increase meaningful and productive academic learning for all learners.
b. For learners to write notes and class activities given by the teacher.
c. For learners to be quiet throughout the lesson.
d. For the teacher to show authority.
a. To increase meaningful and productive academic learning for all learners.

Which of the following statements best describes the use of computer-aided design (CAD)?
Select one:
a. To analyze and interpret data from experiments.
b. To create designs using hand-drawn sketches.
c. To design and simulate 3D models of objects.
d. To write and edit computer programs.
c. To design and simulate 3D models of objects.

According to technology, what are resources?
Select one:
a. Branded, expensive school uniform, school bags and badges.
b. Well trained, motivated teachers and school staff.
c. Learning material that replace appropriate and effective methods of learning for the sake of novelty or fashion.
d. Things that replace teachers or undermine their integrity and dignity.
c. Learning material that replace appropriate and effective methods of learning for the sake of novelty or fashion.

Which of the following is NOT a facility resource example
Select one:
a. Laboratory
b. Classroom
c. Workshop
d. Lesson plan
d. Lesson plan

As Technology teachers, how can you gather information where measurement is concerned?
Select one:
a. Using the Zone of Proximal Development
b. Observation
c. Bringing learning materials and equipment in class
d. Using Bloom's taxonomy
b. Observation

Having resources to do your job when you start may be affected by factors such as:
Select one:
a. Learners skipping technology lessons
b. How well the municipal education allocates the budget
c. The teacher not giving learners resources to save them
d. Skipping components that require resources in technology lessons
b. How well the municipal education allocates the budget

As Technology teachers, how can you gather information where measurement is concerned?
Select one:
a. Using the Zone of Proximal Development
b. Observation
c. Bringing learning materials and equipment in class
d. Using Bloom's taxonomy
b. Observation

Having resources to do your job when you start may be affected by factors such as:
Select one:
a. Learners skipping technology lessons
b. How well the municipal education allocates the budget
c. The teacher not giving learners resources to save them
d. Skipping components that require resources in technology lessons
b. How well the municipal education allocates the budget

Which of the following best describes classroom management?
Select one:
a. The process of organizing desks and chairs in a classroom.
b. The practice of controlling students and their behavior in the classroom.
c. The strategies and techniques used by teachers to create and maintain a productive and organized learning environment in their classroom.
d. The implementation of a rigid set of rules and consequences for students
c. The strategies and techniques used by teachers to create and maintain a productive and organized learning environment in their classroom.

Classroom management are NOT effective when:
Select one:
a. They emphasize learners' expectations for behaviour and learning
b. They identify important learner behaviours for success
c. They keep the learner quiet throughout the lesson and allow only the teacher the talk
d. They promote active learning and learner involvement
c. They keep the learner quiet throughout the lesson and allow only the teacher to talk.

How can you manage disciple in a practical teaching environment?
Select one:
a. Use global standards that govern the use of personal protection equipment.
b. Learners should use tools and machines with no supervisor, as long as they know how to use them.
c. Allow learners to create their own rules to feel comfortable.
d. Appoint a class representative and leave them in charge of the practical teaching environment.
d. Appoint a class representative and leave them in charge of the practical teaching environment.

According to the Department of Education, which one is the specific aims of technology?
Select one:
a. For the development and application of specific design skills to solve problems.
b. To improve the standards of living.
c. For learners to be prepared to answer exam and tests questions.
d. To help learners understand calculations.
a. For the development and application of specific design skills to solve problems.

What are the different types of comfort in classroom management?
Select one:
a. Cognitive comfort, teacher and emotional comfort.
b. Teacher and learner comfort.
c. Social comfort, mental comfort and cognitive comfort.
d. Physical comfort, emotional comfort, cognitive comfort, and social comfort.
d. Physical comfort, emotional comfort, cognitive comfort, and social comfort.

Which of the following is not an FET study Field linked to Technology Grades 10-12?
Select one:
a. Life Sciences
b. Design
c. Physical Sciences
d. Mechanical Technology
a. Life Sciences

What is a MINI Practical Assessment Task?
Select one:
a. Activities used to teach and then practice specific skills in preparation for a more advanced task sometimes also called resource tasks.
b. A task which makes up the main formal assessment of a learner's skills and knowledge application during each term.
c. It is a class activity that learners write after a lesson
d. A task that learners do and submit at the end of the year as the portfolio of evidence.
a. Activities used to teach and then practice specific skills in preparation for a more advanced task sometimes also called resource tasks.

What can be considered for creating ideal classrooms?
Select one:
a. Varying the activity- keeping learners on their toes.
b. Class sizes- big classes and leaving the class to work as one big group.
c. Visual clues- using one learning aid for all learners in class.
d. Clean walls- Making sure there are no posters or written cue cards on the wall
a. Varying the activity- keeping learners on their toes.

What is the purpose of an assessments in Technology?
Select one:
a. To measure the effectiveness of ongoing teaching
b. To encourage individual work
c. To help learners to work together with their peers
d. To understand how to use the Zone of Proximal Development
a. To measure the effectiveness of ongoing teaching

What is the purpose of a computer in a technology classroom?
Select one:
a. For learners to play games that are not related to the technology lessons so that they can relax.
b. To decorate the class so that anyone who comes in can tell it's a technology classroom
c. For learners to watch YouTube lessons when the teacher is tired.
d. To teach using dynamic slideshows such as PowerPoint.
d. To teach using dynamic slideshows such as PowerPoint.

As Technology teachers, how can you gather information where measurement is concerned?
Select one:
a. Using Bloom's taxonomy
b. Observation
c. Bringing learning materials and equipment in class
d. Using the Zone of Proximal Development
b. Observation

At the stage of lesson conclusion, what is one of the things that a technology teacher does?
Select one:
a. Prepares to take the lesson further by explaining the concepts.
b. Follows on the additional concepts and content that was introduced, internalized and interpreted.
c. Ask baseline questions to check how much learners know about the subject.
d. Introduce new concepts and go deeper into content.
b. Follows on the additional concepts and content that was introduced, internalized and interpreted.

a. To promote social and communication skills by creating a classroom environment that emphasizes collaboration and exchange of ideas
b. To provide experience in and appreciation for multiple perspectives (evaluation of alternative solutions).
c. To allow learners to learn by doing, applying ideas and solve problems
d. To allow learners to construct knowledge rather than just passively take in information.
All of the options listed (a, b, c, and d) are purposes of Project-Based Learning (PBL).
